free_text: Interaction of high-energy protons and helium ions
with niobium
The energies were believed to be correct within an
uncertainty of +-10 MeV. Beam intensity was of the
order of 0.5 muA.
Thin niobium target foils.
Impurities in the niobium foil were as follows:
Impurity    Percent     Impurity          Percent
Oxygen      0.03        Titanium          0.01
Nitrogen    0.03        Tungsten   less than 0.01
Carbon      0.015       Zirconium  less than 0.005
Tantalum    0.05        Nickel     less than 0.005
Iron        0.015
Independent empirical corrections for self-absorption,
back scattering, etc., were not made.
Estimated over all error of absolute cross
sections.
Data taken from Phys.Rev.136,B425,1964,
Tables III-IV.
DR Reference corrected to follow APS format
OS. Isomer assignments corrected in
subs. 2,4,6,15,17,19. Monitor coding modified in all
subs. Added EN-ERR, updated error analysis.
OS. REACTION in sub.2 modified
Standard error of ratio in case of more
than one determination
Isomer code in DECAY-DATA corrected;
changed SF3 to X in Al-27 reaction,
added pointer to monitor
Added SF5=CUM to Nb93 reaction
Changed SF3 to X in Al-27 reaction,
added MONIT-REF, added pointer to monitor
